message:
fix(status): emit full quarantine report in status --json, not just summary

The quarantine payload rendered by \`cass status --json\` inside
\`run_status\` previously nested the diag-quarantine output under a
\`{\"summary\": ...}\` wrapper and threw away the rest of the
report. That left a real gap: the lockstep test in
\`tests/lifecycle_matrix.rs\` (status/diag/doctor stay in
lockstep, bd-ibuuh.19 companion commit) needs the FULL report
shape — candidates, retained/reclaimable breakdown,
retained_publish_backups, cleanup_apply — to compare across
surfaces. With only the summary, a regression in the other
report fields would silently ship through status while diag and
doctor caught it.

Widen the payload:

- Stop unwrapping \`.summary\` on the return value of
  \`collect_diag_quarantine_report\`. Bind the full report to a
  local \`quarantine_report\` and pass it straight into the JSON
  object under the \`\"quarantine\"\` key.
- The JSON object's \`quarantine\` value flips from
  \`{\"summary\": <summary>}\` to \`<full report>\`, which itself
  has a \`summary\` field. Callers that previously read
  \`quarantine.summary.foo\` continue to work against the new
  shape — the nesting \`quarantine.summary\` path is preserved
  — while every other field the report already surfaces becomes
  visible.

Drive-by: absorb a single rustfmt width rewrap in the adjacent
\`cleanup_target_safety_rejects_hardlink_alias_pointing_at_db_path\`
test so the file stays rustfmt-clean.

No schema migration needed; existing status JSON readers that
keyed on \`quarantine.summary.*\` continue to work unchanged.
